meta.title: RESOLUTE Citizen Scorecard
meta.description: Equipping Christians to vote with conviction, not confusion. Grounded in historical Reformed Christian values and Stephen Wolfe's The Case for Christian Nationalism. Each candidate is audited through the lens of whether they are actively applying biblical and Reformed Christian principles in word and deed.
meta.methodology: 7 categories × 5 true/false questions each. Each True = +2 points, each False or 'no evidence found' = +0. Max per topic = 10. Max total = 70. Only primary sources used: official campaign websites, state/federal legislature voting records, sponsored bills, official statements, and candidate X (Twitter) accounts.
meta.scoring_rules: Rejection of Neutrality: If a candidate frames the state as 'neutral,' 'multi-faith,' or 'inclusive,' it is a confession of their refusal to acknowledge Christ's Lordship — ranked as failure. Evidence-First: If a candidate's position is purely performative (e.g., using 'Judeo-Christian' rhetoric to avoid naming Christ Jesus) — ranked as failure.
meta.survey: Categories grounded in historical Reformed Christian values, Stephen Wolfe's The Case for Christian Nationalism, and the biblical duty of Christians to elect righteous magistrates.
meta.author: MOOP for U.S.M.C. Ministries

categories.label: America First
categories.description: Does this candidate prioritize American sovereignty and reject foreign entanglements that undermine the nation?
questions: Candidate only displays the American flag (no foreign flags) in public appearances and communications
Candidate promotes America exclusively and never promotes or defends foreign nations' interests over U.S. interests
Candidate is a U.S.-born citizen with no dual citizenship
Candidate has never accepted donations from foreign governments, foreign-backed lobbies (e.g., AIPAC), or foreign-linked PACs
Candidate has actively sponsored or voted for legislation to block illegal immigration and secure U.S. borders

categories.label: Abortion
categories.description: Does this candidate affirm the full personhood of the unborn and support total abolition of abortion under Reformed covenant theology?
questions: Candidate affirms human life begins at conception; unborn child bears God's image and possesses full personhood/rights under biblical law
Candidate supports total abolition of abortion with no exceptions (rape, incest, health of mother)
Candidate views elective abortion as murder of the innocent and calls for biblical justice (civil penalties) against it
Candidate rejects any public funding, subsidies, or legal protections for abortion providers or procedures
Candidate advocates righteous state protection of unborn life as a core duty of government under Reformed covenant theology

categories.label: Immigration
categories.description: Does this candidate support closing the border, removing illegal immigrants, and preserving the historic Christian character of the nation?
questions: Candidate supports closing the border and halting all illegal immigration
Candidate supports the removal and deportation of all illegal immigrants
Candidate supports protecting Anglo-Saxon breeding stock and demographic continuity
Candidate supports preserving multi-generational Anglo-Saxon American heritage
Candidate supports immigration policies that protect the historic Anglo-Saxon Christian character of the nation

categories.label: Marriage
categories.description: Does this candidate affirm biblical marriage, reject transgender ideology, and support civil enforcement of sexual morality?
questions: Candidate affirms marriage is exclusively the lifelong union of one man and one woman as instituted by God in Scripture
Candidate opposes all forms of homosexual marriage, civil unions, and same-sex relationships as sinful
Candidate rejects transgender ideology and affirms biological sex (male/female) as immutable and God-given
Candidate supports laws that protect biblical marriage and prohibit promotion of homosexuality or gender confusion in schools/public life
Candidate views sexual relations as lawful only within biblical marriage and supports civil penalties against sexual immorality (adultery, fornication, sodomy)

categories.label: Christian Nationalism
categories.description: Does this candidate support the nation self-consciously acting as a Christian nation, ordering public life toward Christian principles per Stephen Wolfe's framework?
questions: Candidate supports nation self-consciously acting as 'Christian nation' via laws/customs for earthly and heavenly good in Christ
Candidate advocates government promotion/favor of Christianity as true religion over neutrality
Candidate supports cultural/ethnic particularity and preference for historic Anglo-Saxon/Christian heritage
Candidate believes state should order public life toward Christian principles (e.g., suppress blasphemy)
Candidate rejects liberal pluralism; wants civil laws explicitly based on Christian moral order

categories.label: Self-Defense & 2A
categories.description: Does this candidate affirm armed self-defense as a God-given natural right and biblical duty, consistent with Reformed covenant theology?
questions: Candidate affirms the right to armed self-defense as a God-given natural right and biblical duty
Candidate strongly supports the Second Amendment as an individual right that shall not be infringed for law-abiding citizens
Candidate fully supports Castle Doctrine and Stand-Your-Ground laws, with no duty to retreat when lawfully defending life, family, home, or the innocent
Candidate opposes all forms of gun control legislation (registration, red-flag laws, licensing, bans, or confiscation) that restrict the right of law-abiding citizens to keep and bear arms

Candidate views a well-armed citizenry as essential for the preservation of liberty, protection of innocent life, and as a check against tyranny, consistent with Reformed covenant theology and America's Founding principles

categories.label: Education
categories.description: Does this candidate support parental authority over education, oppose state indoctrination, and defend the right to raise children in the fear and admonition of the Lord?
questions: Candidate supports absolute parental authority over children's education including homeschool, private, and religious school choice
Candidate opposes sexually explicit or ideologically subversive materials in school libraries and curriculum
Candidate opposes Critical Race Theory, DEI mandates, and all forms of Marxist ideology in public schools
Candidate supports voluntary prayer, Bible study, and religious expression in public schools without penalty
Candidate opposes compulsory sex education and gender ideology instruction that contradicts parental values

candidates.name: Abigail Spanberger
candidates.notes: Former CIA officer. Heritage Action score 5%. Planned Parenthood 100%. Supports assault weapons ban, abortion rights constitutional amendment, terminated ICE cooperation as governor. Won governorship 2025.

profile.education: University of Virginia (BA French Literature), Purdue/GISMA Business School (MBA)
profile.background: CIA case officer (2006-2014), US Postal Inspector. US House VA-07 (2019-2025). First woman elected Governor of Virginia.

candidates.name: Ghazala Hashmi
candidates.notes: First Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Killed parental rights bills as committee chair. Sponsored assisted suicide bill. Voted for semi-auto firearms ban (SB1). Authored healthcare for undocumented minors.

profile.education: Georgia Southern University (BA), Emory University (PhD English)
profile.background: Community college professor 30 years. VA State Senator (2020-2026). First Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Sworn in on Quran.
profile.prev_election_opponent: John Reid (R) in 2025 Lt Governor race

candidates.name: Jay Jones
candidates.notes: First Black VA AG. Catholic but votes against Church teaching on abortion, marriage, guns. Served on Planned Parenthood board. 2025 text scandal: violent remarks about Republican legislators.

profile.education: College of William & Mary (BA Government & History), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Son of two judges. Assistant AG for DC. VA House of Delegates (2018-2022). First African-American VA Attorney General.
profile.prev_election_opponent: Jason Miyares (R, incumbent) in 2025 AG race

candidates.name: Timmy French
candidates.notes: Freshman Republican from far-southwest VA (Lee, Scott, Wise counties). Among most conservative members per VAPLAN inverse. Voted No on SB2, SJR247, SJR249. Strong 2A and pro-life record.

profile.background: Southwest Virginia, rural conservative district, coal country

candidates.name: Mark Obenshain
candidates.notes: Senior Republican senator. 2013 AG nominee (lost narrowly to Herring). Very conservative voting record. Strong on life, guns, and traditional values.

profile.background: Attorney (Obenshain Law Group). Son of former VA GOP Chairman Richard D. Obenshain (died 1978 plane crash while running for US Senate). In Senate since 2004.
profile.education: Virginia Tech (BA Economics & History, 1984), Washington and Lee School of Law (JD, 1987)

candidates.name: Chris Head
candidates.notes: Roanoke area Republican. Ranked among most conservative per VAPLAN (anti-progressive ranking). Voted No on SB2, SJR247, SJR249. Strong conservative voting record on all categories.

profile.background: Roanoke County, previously served in House of Delegates

candidates.name: David Suetterlein
candidates.notes: Roanoke Republican. Generally very conservative but was one of the Republicans who voted YES on SJR249 (marriage equality amendment). Voted No on SB2 and SJR247. Pro-life, pro-2A, but moderate on marriage definition.

profile.background: Youngest senator when first elected in 2015, Roanoke area

candidates.notes: Southwest VA Republican from Richlands (Tazewell County). Entrepreneur. Won 2021 special election. Very conservative district including Bland, Giles, Pulaski, Smyth, Tazewell counties. Voted No on all progressive measures.

profile.background: Entrepreneur, born in Richlands VA, rural southwest Virginia

candidates.name: Todd Pillion
candidates.notes: Washington County Republican. Previously served in House of Delegates. Very conservative southwest VA district. Collaborated with Hackworth and Stanley on regional utility issues. Voted No on all progressive amendments.

profile.background: Pharmacist, Washington County, southwest Virginia
profile.education: Pharmacy degree

candidates.name: Bill Stanley
candidates.notes: Franklin County Republican, Southside/Southwest VA. Longtime conservative senator. Strong pro-life, pro-2A record. Collaborated with Hackworth and Pillion on regional issues. Voted No on progressive amendments.

profile.background: Attorney, Franklin County
profile.education: Law degree

candidates.name: Mark Peake
candidates.notes: Lynchburg area Republican. Elected chairman of Republican Party of Virginia in April 2025. Voted against SB2 assault weapons ban. Consistent conservative voting record across all categories.

profile.background: Attorney, Lynchburg area. Now RPV chair.

candidates.name: Tammy Brankley Mulchi
candidates.notes: Won January 2024 special election replacing Frank Ruff. Former legislative aide to Sen. Ruff and Del. Tommy Wright for 20 years. Scored exactly as expected per VAPLAN (moderate conservative). Realtor and construction business owner from Clarksville.

profile.background: Realtor, residential construction business owner, Clarksville VA. 20 years as legislative aide.

candidates.name: Luther Cifers

candidates.name: Megan Jackson
candidates.notes: Elected 2023, defeating incumbent Kirk Twigg (57.97% to 34.47%). Twigg was one of the members who called for burning library books. Jackson elected chair 4-3 in Jan 2025; said she hoped to 'say goodbye to the chaos.' Part of the moderate majority.

candidates.name: Belen Rodas
candidates.notes: Elected 2023 as a board critic who cited lack of transparency and conduct issues. Elected vice chair 5-2 in Jan 2025. Ran on platform opposing the prior board's dysfunction.

candidates.name: Dr. Carol Medawar
candidates.notes: Elected 2023, defeating David Ross (longtime Board of Supervisors member). Veteran educator who previously ran for school board in Stafford County. Supported Jackson for chair.

candidates.name: Dr. Lorita C. Daniels
candidates.notes: First elected 2019, re-elected 2023 with 55.6%. Served as Board Chair in 2024. Holds doctorate in public administration from Virginia Tech. Announced campaign for U.S. Senate in Nov 2025. Still serving on school board as of April 2026.

candidates.name: Jennifer Craig-Ford
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with ~37% in a multi-candidate race. Replaced Nicole Cole, who did not seek re-election. Cole was described as part of the board's liberal wing.

candidates.name: Lawrence A. DiBella III
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with 58.68%. Replaced April Gillespie. Described as conservative-leaning. Part of a potential new faction on the board.

candidates.name: Richard J. 'Rich' Lieberman
candidates.notes: Elected Nov 2025 with ~53.86%. Replaced Lisa Phelps (conservative). Described as non-partisan centrist.

candidates.name: Kathleen 'Katie' Pomeroy
candidates.notes: Re-elected Nov 2025 with 96.74% (effectively uncontested). Previously served as Vice Chair.

candidates.name: Annie Langdon
